text.title: CSS-in-JS Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the CSS-in-JS Basics methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Style props MUST be typed (e.g. `variant: 'primary' | 'secondary'`). String-only style props are rejected.
Theme tokens MUST flow through the library's theme provider, not be imported as constants. Direct imports are rejected.
rule.rationale: Theme switching breaks when tokens are statically imported.

rule.statement: Every primitive carries a `displayName`. Anonymous styled components are rejected.
Style nesting MUST cap at 3 levels. Deeper nesting is rejected.
Global styles (`createGlobalStyle`) MUST be scoped to namespaces, not strewn across the codebase.
rule.rationale: Global CSS sprawl is the #1 regression source.

rule.statement: Components MUST work under SSR; usage of `window` / `document` at module-level is rejected.
rule.rationale: SSR-blocking imports are easy to add and hard to find later.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running CSS-in-JS Basics under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
